last_result.effect: This asks the manager for information only; it does not change the EasyCrypt proof state.
last_result.result: The manager returned read-only context for the current goal.

items.candidate: call (_: inv ‹ROin.m | ROout.m | ROF.m›{1} ‹ROin.m | ROout.m | ROF.m›{2} ‹ROin.m | ROout.m | ROF.m›{1} ‹ROin.m | ROout.m | ROF.m›{2} Mem.log{1} Mem.log{2} Mem.lc{1} Mem.lc{2} BNR.lenc{1} BNR.lenc{2} ‹BNR.ndec | UFCMA.cbad1 | UFCMA.cbad2 | UFCMA4.cforged | UFCMA_li.i | UFCMA_li.cbadi›{1} ‹BNR.ndec | UFCMA.cbad1 | UFCMA.cbad2 | UFCMA4.cforged | UFCMA_li.i | UFCMA_li.cbadi›{2} ‹ROin.m | ROout.m | ROF.m›{1}).

No mechanical `={glob}` frame applies at this call — build the call invariant from THIS development's own in-scope predicates (an invariant conjunct can be a guarded implication / size-or-count bound / domain fact, not only an equality). In-scope forms + named predicates (use lookup_symbol for the full definition of any): guarded implication (guard => relation) — inv_cpa: log1 = log2 /\ lenc1 = lenc2 /\ lc1 = lc2 /\ ndec1 = ndec2 /\ (forall n c, (n,c) \in mr1 => n \in lenc1) /\ (forall n c, (n,c) \in ms1 => n \in lenc1). local eq; make_lbad1: flatten (map (f | size / count bound — check_plaintext: let (n, a, m) = p in ! n \in lenc /\ valid_topol a m /\ size lenc < qenc. op check_cipher (ndec:int) (c:ciphertext) = (let (n, a, m, t) = c in valid_topol a m) ; inv: inv_cpa mr1 ms1 log1 log2 lc1 lc2 l | domain membership — check_plaintext: let (n, a, m) = p in ! n \in lenc /\ valid_topol a m /\ size lenc < qenc. op check_cipher (ndec:int) (c:ciphertext) = (let (n, a, m, t) = c in valid_topol a m) ; inv: inv_cpa mr1 ms1 log1 log2 lc1 lc2 le | relational equality — check_cipher: (let (n, a, m, t) = c in valid_topol a m) /\ ndec < qdec. (* Bounded and Nonce Respecting *) module BNR (O:CCA_Oracles) = { var lenc : nonce list var ndec : int

source.label: Parsed from EasyCrypt pretty goal text

moves.applicability: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.

moves.tactic_shape: Inspect proof obligation stack: local_procedure_call / current_frontier / A.main / statement 2.

Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.

moves.title: Invariant-call route
moves.applicability: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.
moves.needs_frame: Postcondition shape only — no `={...}` equality frame. Build the `={...}` part from `application_context.write_map` (per candidate field: who writes it — the read-only/preserved fields are safe to equate), then add your coupling before this is a usable invariant.

frontier_alignment.summary: Frontier alignment: setup statements appear before the current frontier; call sites are visible at the frontier. Use this as a proof-reader map before choosing while/rnd/seq/call tactics.

first_instruction_alignment.proof_read: The first visible statements have different heads; align or consume setup statements before using a symmetric branch/loop/call tactic.

rows.proof_read: These are setup statements before the current frontier; preserve the proof-relevant initialized state in later invariants, seq cuts, or calls.
Both sides expose the same call procedure; a named equiv call or an invariant call may apply at this frontier.

procedure_navigation.how_to_use: Positions for choosing wp/sp depth, which `if` to rcond, and where the next loop/call frontier is. Facts only — pick the tactic and the branch direction yourself.

absorb_depth.sp_hint: sp 1 1 / wp -1 -1 absorbs the straight-line setup (1 left, 1 right) before the next frontier

checks: Diagnostics locate the frontier; do not infer a proof script from an error.

application_context.how_to_use: Use this section when constructing the concrete invariant inside `call (_: ...)`. This section is not a tactic. It is a checklist for constructing the concrete invariant before using `probe_tactic` or `commit_tactic`.

inspect_if_unsure.why: After you draft a concrete invariant, this preview shows the obligations it creates and can reveal missing facts or extra conjuncts that make the proof harder.

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

notes.message: proof-state analysis layer is call_site: 2 call site(s) and 1 callable lemma handle(s) are live (CCP_OCCP). `inline *` is a broad global transformation here and can hide those handles; named calls and targeted inline are route context for comparison.
Some imported or cross-scope handles may need lookup if that route is being tested; use lookup_candidates for the concrete symbol instead of treating the whole list as a to-do queue.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: A tactic or probe failed and the latest error needs classification.
Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.
Need the cross-step proof timeline or confusion history.

lookup_candidates.use_when: Resolve exact signature for `CCP_OCCP` only when this handle route is being tested.
